failed_sources.reason: Round Rock ISD 2024 Bond Tracker: old URL redirects to a JS-rendered SPA; automated fetch could not confirm turf/sport content this run. Reviewer confidence 0.25 (rejected).
Round Rock ISD Purchasing page: HTTP 404, no verified replacement at a guessed path. Superseded by SRC-008 (texasbids.net aggregator).

gaps.note: Own-site 2023 bond virtual-open-house page (Southeast Metro Park turf soccer/softball, Bee Creek Sports Complex) named sports explicitly but is marked expired/stale (confidence 0.35). TexasBids aggregator mirror shows 70+ live solicitations but none turf/sport-specific (confidence 0.38). Recommend next: county's live Parks CIP/ArcGIS project tracker or a dated news item on Bee Creek Phase II (reported spring 2027 completion).

Bid Opportunities page is live/current but no turf listing (confidence 0.5). 'Our Parks' facility inventory confirms baseball/soccer/football fields at Cedar Creek Park but is a historical-reference inventory, not a monitorable procurement/plan channel. Recommend cooperative/aggregator escalation next run.

Bid Requests page is live/current but generic (medical services, restroom remodel, road materials); no turf/sport content (confidence 0.32). Rural county unlikely to be a direct turf procurer; deprioritize vs. its incorporated cities/ISDs next run.

Current-bids page documents a completed 2020 baseball/softball turf bid (confidence 0.5); athletics bond-updates page names baseball/softball but no turf wording and no date (confidence 0.4). Recommend the district's live Bonfire portal with a rendering-capable check, or the High School #2 (Whisper Valley) construction updates once athletic facilities are announced.

2019 bond turf page names football/soccer/baseball/softball but that work is ~6 years old (confidence 0.32); a Jan 2024 bond-purchase news post referenced in its own headline could not be confirmed to include the turf line item on re-fetch. Recommend the 2023 Bond Prop B Chaparral Stadium page or the Long-Range Facility Planning Committee's forthcoming recommendations.

Purchasing portal is live and current (Euna/Bonfire, 16+ cooperative affiliations) but generic, no sport named (confidence 0.52). A completed TDLR-registered baseball/softball fields project (2022-2024) exists but is a closed compliance record, not an ongoing monitor source. Recommend the Facilities Advisory Committee's bond-recommendation output once published.

Required-postings page is actively maintained but its only turf reference is a stale 2022 CMAR RFP with no follow-up (confidence 0.28). Recommend checking for a next bond cycle or the district's Long-Range Facilities Plan for a fresher signal.

A Sept 2025 news post confirms turf installed at two high schools but never names a specific sport (confidence 0.35); the purchasing portal landing page is a generic gateway with no visible current listings (confidence 0.28). Recommend the district's Bond 2022 itemized project list directly rather than a news recap.

TexasBids aggregator lists a real, sport-explicit 'HS#4 new football/baseball/softball fields' package but the listing is 15+ months stale (confidence 0.34); Bond 2024 overview page names only track repairs and a field house building, no turf/sport (confidence 0.32). Recommend re-checking the aggregator listing for a fresher HS#4 construction package next cycle.

Bond 2023 overview funds a new comprehensive high school but names no sport/turf (confidence 0.30); Bonfire procurement portal is live but JS-rendered with no confirmable listing (confidence 0.30). Recommend the 2019 Bond subpage (funded the Hutto Memorial Stadium turf renovation per district history) or a rendering-capable Bonfire check.

2021 bond baseball/softball turf project is explicit but already completed (Aug 2024), so it's historical, not an active opportunity (confidence 0.32). A 2023-bond CMAR RFP for a new middle school explicitly scoped a football practice field (ConstructConnect, paywalled, already awarded/closed). Recommend watching the 2023 Bond Construction Updates hub for a future stadium/field package.

gaps.entity: Texas SmartBuy / Electronic State Business Daily (ESBD)
gaps.note: Live, no-login, highly filterable statewide solicitation search confirmed, but the URL is a bare search entry point rather than a turf-keyword-parameterized deep link, so sport is not confirmed by default (confidence 0.42). Recommend constructing and saving a keyword-filtered URL (e.g. 'artificial turf') if the platform supports shareable filtered links.

gaps.entity: Remaining Travis/Hays/Bastrop/Caldwell school districts (12): Hays CISD, San Marcos CISD, Dripping Springs ISD, Wimberley ISD, Bastrop ISD, Smithville ISD, McDade ISD, Elgin ISD, Lockhart ISD, Luling ISD, Prairie Lea ISD, Liberty Hill ISD
gaps.note: Enumerated in Phase A scoping (locations/registry.yaml scope + web research) but not yet scouted this run -- budget exhausted before reaching them. ISDs have been this run's highest-yield entity type (8 of 9 confident sources); prioritize these first next run.

gaps.entity: Remaining incorporated cities (~23): Cedar Park, San Marcos, Kyle, Buda, Dripping Springs, Wimberley, Bastrop, Elgin, Smithville, Lockhart, Luling, Manor, Bee Cave, Lakeway, Lago Vista, Jonestown, Hutto, Taylor, Liberty Hill, Georgetown, and remaining minor municipalities
gaps.note: Enumerated in Phase A scoping but not yet scouted this run -- budget exhausted. Recommend prioritizing county-seat and larger cities (Georgetown, San Marcos, Kyle, Buda) next given parks-department capital-project capacity observed elsewhere in this MSA.

gaps.entity: Regional aggregators and purchasing cooperatives: BidNet Direct (Texas Purchasing Group), BuyBoard, TIPS, Choice Partners
gaps.note: Identified in Phase A research as Texas-relevant but not scouted as standalone entities this run (BidNet Direct and texasbids.net were used opportunistically as candidate sources for individual entities instead). Recommend a dedicated scout pass per cooperative next run, since a single cooperative bid can span many districts in this MSA at once.
